Plectranthus rosmarinifolius, also known as Piofontia rosmarinifolia (or Coleus rosmarinifolius), is a plant species in the Lamiaceae family, commonly known as a type of mint or verbena. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, headache, and respiratory issues, due to its antibacterial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Plectranthus rosmarinifolius include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Plectranthus rosmarinifolius include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of health conditions.
